title: Oeceoclades saundersiana
taxonomy: Oeceoclades saundersiana is a species of orchid in the genus Oeceoclades. The genus Oeceoclades is a part of the subtribe Craniophorinae and the tribe Cranichideae within the subfamily Orchidoideae of the family Orchidaceae. This classification places Oeceoclades saundersiana in the larger orchid family. The exact classification can be broken down into: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Monocots, Order: Asparagales, Family: Orchidaceae, Subfamily: Orchidoideae, Tribe: Cranichideae, Subtribe: Craniophorinae, Genus: Oeceoclades, Species: O. saundersiana. This detailed classification helps identify the plant's unique characteristics and its position within the plant kingdom.
uses: Oeceoclades saundersiana is a species of orchid that has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in the treatment of various ailments. In some African cultures, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
